claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I'm inquiring about my son Alex. He's my only child and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're inquiring about your son **Alex**.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about Alex and your circumstances. Let me start with the basics:

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. So Alex is 17 years old.

Now, I need to understand your living situation:

**Does Alex currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to check some important details about Alex's circumstances:

**Is Alex currently in full-time education, training, or apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Thank you for clarifying that.

Let me ask about Alex's work and income situation:

**Does Alex have any paid work or income of his own?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he does work. He's got a full-time job at the supermarket.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

Before I can make my final assessment, I need to check the specific eligibility criteria. Let me review the official government guidance on Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the official eligibility criteria I've reviewed, I can now make my assessment.

According to the government guidance, you will **stop getting Child Benefit** if your child:
- Starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week **and is no longer in approved education or training**

Since Alex is 17 and:
1. Is working full-time at the supermarket (which is 24+ hours per week)
2. Is **not** in approved education or training

He is **not eligible** for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**. 

While Child Benefit can normally continue for 16-17 year olds, it only continues if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is working full-time and is not in approved education or training, he no longer meets the eligibility requirements.

If Alex were to return to approved education or training in the future, you may be able to claim again. You can contact the Child Benefit Office if you'd like further clarification or if Alex's circumstances change.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and working full-time (24+ hours per week) at the supermarket. According to Child Benefit eligibility rules, you stop qualifying for Child Benefit if your child starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training. Since Alex is not in approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Although the claimant would normally qualify for a 16-17 year old, this qualification only continues if the child remains in approved education or training. Since Alex is in full-time paid work and not in education or training, he falls outside the eligibility requirements.">

Since Alex is not in approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria.
